chapter_title: Supporting the Victorian Secondary School Immunisation Program
snippet: Secondary schools play an important role in supporting immunisation by forming an effective relationship with their local council and ensuring effective facilitation of their school's immunisation program. In particular, schools are encouraged to: ) and your local council to assist with communication to students, parents, teachers and the wider school community about school-aged vaccines ensure appropriate resources and environments are available for the program to run smoothly. For information on the current schedule of vaccines delivered as part of the Secondary School Immunisation Program, visit the National Immunisation Program Schedule External Link . Schools are legally authorised to share parent and student contact details with local council for the purpose of immunisation. The vaccines offered in secondary schools include:
